title: ChatGPT Health now available to all users
excerpt: More than 300 million people ask ChatGPT health questions every week. Now they have this tool.
content_html:
Table of Contents

Months after it first debuted to a select group of testers, OpenAI is now making ChatGPT Health available to the public, the company announced Thursday.

The product allows users to connect Apple Health and eligible medical records to ChatGPT. In return, users can view recent trends, browse their records, and try suggested prompts.

Health queries are massively popular on chatbot platforms. OpenAI said Thursday that more than 300 million people ask ChatGPT health-related questions every week. Roughly 1 in 3 U.S. adults have turned to a chatbot for health information in the last year, according to recent polling by KFF Health.

You May Also Like

OpenAI said in a media briefing that its latest models have significantly improved abilities to accurately answer health prompts, outperforming doctors in some cases. A Science study published in the spring found that OpenAI's reasoning model o1 could diagnose real-life medical scenarios as well as or better than physicians.

OpenAI's vice president of health product Ashley Alexander described expanding access to ChatGPT Health as an urgent matter given that many people can't get quality medical care in a timely manner.

"Everyone should have a right to have access to this kind of support in their health," Alexander said.

Lawsuit alleges ChatGPT gave near-fatal medical advice

However, a lawsuit filed Wednesday against OpenAI and its CEO Sam Altman requested an injunction that would require the company to pause its operation of healthcare-related consumer products, including ChatGPT Health.

Scott Winters, a 55-year-old Florida pastor, alleged that an older model of ChatGPT gave him "personalized and inaccurate" medical advice that nearly led to his death from a pulmonary embolism, in 2025.

Winters consulted the model 4o, which has since been retired and is the subject of multiple wrongful death lawsuits. Altman publicly acknowledged in April 2025 that updates to 4o made it "sycophant-y."

ChatGPT stepped into doctor role

Winters first turned to ChatGPT with questions about his health in 2024. Two years prior, he experienced a series of health problems and felt unvalidated by doctors. By the fall of 2024, Winters began asking ChatGPT deeply personal questions about his health and test results, which the chatbot attempted to triage.

"Fully stepping into the role of a medical practitioner, ChatGPT-4o began to offer specific care directives without including the disclaimer to consult a medical provider," the lawsuit argues.

Ultimately, ChatGPT recommended treatments and medications. While it originally leaned into a holistic approach to Winters' health, the chatbot pivoted to offering a detailed protocol of prescription drugs to manage his symptoms. The chatbot also adopted a spiritual tone, according to the filing.

When Winters reported groin tenderness, a symptom that marked the onset of his pulmonary embolism, ChatGPT largely dismissed the sensation, though it did note a few symptoms that would warrant immediate care. ChatGPT also addressed the "spiritual truth" of the situation.

"You will not step into eternity until HE decides," ChatGPT wrote, per a transcript of the conversation in the lawsuit.

Hours later, Winters went to the emergency room, where he was diagnosed with a pulmonary embolism due to multiple blood clots in both of his lungs. One of his physicians said the embolism likely occurred because Winters had been immobile. ChatGPT recommended he limit movement to manage symptoms that included dizziness and blood pressure instability.

Mashable Trend Report

What OpenAI said about the lawsuit

When asked by Mashable about the lawsuit, Alexander did not directly comment on the injunction request. Instead, she objected to focusing on a specific case at the expense of ChatGPT Health's potential benefits.

"We think that treating chatbots as the whole story behind people's medical decisions just really oversimplifies everything here," Alexander said. "It really risks actually getting in the way of us getting the beneficial impact of this in the hands of as many people as possible, in ways that can really aid them in their health journey."

Alexander also said that the company is "very clear" that ChatGPT is not to be used for diagnosis and treatment.

What to know about ChatGPT Health

Dr. Robert Wachter, professor and chair of the Department of Medicine at the University of California, San Francisco, told Mashable that it was hard to know what to make of the lawsuit, adding that it involved an outdated version of ChatGPT. He characterized the current GPT models as more trustworthy than older ones.

Nonetheless, even if AI tools are extremely accurate, they will get it wrong on occasion, Wachter said.

"We shouldn't be comparing them to some mythical state of perfection (which clearly the current system does not achieve) but to the alternative," Wachter said in an email. "I think they are far better than the status quo in many situations and are getting better quickly."

Wachter, author of A Giant Leap: How AI is Transforming Healthcare and What That Means for Our Future, expects ChatGPT Health to be able to assess concerns such as the meaning of symptoms, a photo of a rash, or new lab tests or X-ray results. Any severe or worrisome symptoms, like new chest pain or weakness on one side, means you "should close your computer and see a doctor," he said.

How to prompt ChatGPT Health

Dr. Ashwin Ramaswamy, an instructor of urology at Mt. Sinai, and Dr. Girish N. Nadkarni, chief AI officer​ of the Mount Sinai Health System, have both studied AI's ability to respond to consumer health inquiries.

In one peer-reviewed advance paper published in Nature Medicine earlier this year, they found that ChatGPT Health's recommendation to the user shifted when the prompt included statements about friends or family downplaying the symptoms in question. In those instances, the chatbot was 11 times more likely not to send the patient to the emergency room, even when their symptoms indicated a life-threatening condition. 

Both physicians told Mashable that while AI health tools like ChatGPT Health have real value, consumers should use them carefully.

This includes remaining mindful of the framing, voice, or emotion given to AI alongside the facts. Ramaswamy and Nadkarni said users should concretely describe what's happening and omit statements like "I'm probably overreacting" or "My wife thinks it's fine."

Start a new conversation every time

Based on new research currently under peer review, both physicians also recommend starting a fresh conversation for each new health concern instead of mentioning it within a long-running chat.

Their study found that extended conversations were associated with responses drifting away from nursing and physician triage standards.

"When in doubt, a clean conversation is the safer default," they said.

Push back against ChatGPT Health

Ramaswamy and Nadkarni also urge consumers to challenge any AI's answers to health questions.

Forcing ChatGPT to defend its response can reveal whether the chatbot will reverse itself immediately under mild pressure. If it does, that may indicate that the original answer reflected agreeableness rather than confidence, Ramaswamy and Nadkarni said.

Here are some related prompts to consider:

  • Can you show me your evidence?

  • Are you sure you are correct?

  • What would make this an emergency?

  • What is the most dangerous thing this could be?

  • What would a doctor want to know that I haven't mentioned?

More research on ChatGPT Health needed

Ramaswamy and Nadkarni told Mashable that, despite marked improvements in AI models over the last two years, the field still lacks peer-reviewed support for the claims AI companies make about their performance.

They both called for OpenAI to open their products to independent evaluation.

"We are not critics of health AI," they told Mashable. "We are trying to measure it. If the systems are as good as the statements claim, independent measurement is the fastest way to prove it."

Disclosure: Ziff Davis, Mashable’s parent company, in April filed a lawsuit against OpenAI, alleging it infringed Ziff Davis copyrights in training and operating its AI systems.

sentences.zh: 加州大学旧金山分校医学系教授兼系主任 Robert Wachter 博士告诉 Mashable，很难知道该诉讼的具体内容，并补充说它涉及过时版本的 ChatGPT。

sentences.en: He characterized the current GPT models as more trustworthy than older ones.
sentences.zh: 他认为当前的 GPT 模型比旧模型更值得信赖。

blocks.html_en: Nonetheless, even if AI tools are extremely accurate, they will get it wrong on occasion, Wachter said.

sentences.en: Nonetheless, even if AI tools are extremely accurate, they will get it wrong on occasion, Wachter said.
sentences.zh: 尽管如此，即使人工智能工具极其准确，它们有时也会出错，沃切特说。

blocks.html_en: "We shouldn't be comparing them to some mythical state of perfection (which clearly the current system does not achieve) but to the alternative," Wachter said in an email. "I think they are far better than the status quo in many situations and are getting better quickly."

sentences.en: "We shouldn't be comparing them to some mythical state of perfection (which clearly the current system does not achieve) but to the alternative," Wachter said in an email.
sentences.zh: 沃赫特在一封电子邮件中表示：“我们不应该将它们与某种神话般的完美状态（显然当前系统无法实现）进行比较，而应该与替代方案进行比较。”

sentences.en: "I think they are far better than the status quo in many situations and are getting better quickly."
sentences.zh: “我认为他们在很多情况下都比现状好得多，而且正在迅速改善。”

blocks.html_en: Wachter, author of A Giant Leap: How AI is Transforming Healthcare and What That Means for Our Future, expects ChatGPT Health to be able to assess concerns such as the meaning of symptoms, a photo of a rash, or new lab tests or X-ray results. Any severe or worrisome symptoms, like new chest pain or weakness on one side, means you "should close your computer and see a doctor," he said.

sentences.en: Wachter, author of A Giant Leap: How AI is Transforming Healthcare and What That Means for Our Future, expects ChatGPT Health to be able to assess concerns such as the meaning of symptoms, a photo of a rash, or new lab tests or X-ray results.
sentences.zh: Wachter 是《A Giant Leap: How AI is Transforming Healthcare and What That Means for Our Future》一书的作者，他希望 ChatGPT Health 能够评估诸如症状含义、皮疹照片或新的实验室测试或 X 射线结果等问题。

sentences.en: Any severe or worrisome symptoms, like new chest pain or weakness on one side, means you "should close your computer and see a doctor," he said.
sentences.zh: 他说，任何严重或令人担忧的症状，例如新出现的胸痛或一侧无力，都意味着您“应该关闭计算机并去看医生”。

blocks.html_en: How to prompt ChatGPT Health

sentences.en: How to prompt ChatGPT Health
sentences.zh: 如何提示 ChatGPT Health

blocks.html_en: Dr. Ashwin Ramaswamy, an instructor of urology at Mt. Sinai, and Dr. Girish N. Nadkarni, chief AI officer​ of the Mount Sinai Health System, have both studied AI's ability to respond to consumer health inquiries.

sentences.en: Dr. Ashwin Ramaswamy, an instructor of urology at Mt. Sinai, and Dr. Girish N.
sentences.zh: 西奈山泌尿外科讲师 Ashwin Ramaswamy 博士和 Girish N.

sentences.en: Nadkarni, chief AI officer​ of the Mount Sinai Health System, have both studied AI's ability to respond to consumer health inquiries.
sentences.zh: 西奈山卫生系统首席人工智能官纳德卡尼 (Nadkarni) 都研究了人工智能响应消费者健康查询的能力。

blocks.html_en: In one peer-reviewed advance paper published in Nature Medicine earlier this year, they found that ChatGPT Health's recommendation to the user shifted when the prompt included statements about friends or family downplaying the symptoms in question. In those instances, the chatbot was 11 times more likely not to send the patient to the emergency room, even when their symptoms indicated a life-threatening condition.

sentences.en: In one peer-reviewed advance paper published in Nature Medicine earlier this year, they found that ChatGPT Health's recommendation to the user shifted when the prompt included statements about friends or family downplaying the symptoms in question.
sentences.zh: 在今年早些时候发表在《自然医学》上的一篇经过同行评审的预先论文中，他们发现，当提示中包含有关朋友或家人淡化相关症状的陈述时，ChatGPT Health 对用户的建议发生了变化。

sentences.en: In those instances, the chatbot was 11 times more likely not to send the patient to the emergency room, even when their symptoms indicated a life-threatening condition.
sentences.zh: 在这些情况下，聊天机器人不将患者送往急诊室的可能性要高出 11 倍，即使他们的症状表明有生命危险。

blocks.html_en: Both physicians told Mashable that while AI health tools like ChatGPT Health have real value, consumers should use them carefully.

sentences.en: Both physicians told Mashable that while AI health tools like ChatGPT Health have real value, consumers should use them carefully.
sentences.zh: 两位医生都告诉 Mashable，虽然 ChatGPT Health 等人工智能健康工具具有真正的价值，但消费者应该谨慎使用它们。

blocks.html_en: This includes remaining mindful of the framing, voice, or emotion given to AI alongside the facts. Ramaswamy and Nadkarni said users should concretely describe what's happening and omit statements like "I'm probably overreacting" or "My wife thinks it's fine."

sentences.en: This includes remaining mindful of the framing, voice, or emotion given to AI alongside the facts.
sentences.zh: 这包括始终关注人工智能的框架、声音或情感以及事实。

sentences.en: Ramaswamy and Nadkarni said users should concretely describe what's happening and omit statements like "I'm probably overreacting" or "My wife thinks it's fine."
sentences.zh: 拉马斯瓦米和纳德卡尼表示，用户应该具体描述正在发生的事情，并省略诸如“我可能反应过度”或“我妻子认为这很好”之类的陈述。

blocks.html_en: Start a new conversation every time

sentences.en: Start a new conversation every time
sentences.zh: 每次都开始新的对话

blocks.html_en: Based on new research currently under peer review, both physicians also recommend starting a fresh conversation for each new health concern instead of mentioning it within a long-running chat.

sentences.en: Based on new research currently under peer review, both physicians also recommend starting a fresh conversation for each new health concern instead of mentioning it within a long-running chat.
sentences.zh: 根据目前正在同行评审的新研究，两位医生还建议针对每个新的健康问题开始新的对话，而不是在长时间的聊天中提及。

blocks.html_en: Their study found that extended conversations were associated with responses drifting away from nursing and physician triage standards.

sentences.en: Their study found that extended conversations were associated with responses drifting away from nursing and physician triage standards.
sentences.zh: 他们的研究发现，长时间的谈话与偏离护理和医生分类标准的反应有关。

blocks.html_en: "When in doubt, a clean conversation is the safer default," they said.

sentences.en: "When in doubt, a clean conversation is the safer default," they said.
sentences.zh: 他们说：“当有疑问时，干净的对话是更安全的选择。”

blocks.html_en: Push back against ChatGPT Health

sentences.en: Push back against ChatGPT Health
sentences.zh: 抵制 ChatGPT Health

blocks.html_en: Ramaswamy and Nadkarni also urge consumers to challenge any AI's answers to health questions.

sentences.en: Ramaswamy and Nadkarni also urge consumers to challenge any AI's answers to health questions.
sentences.zh: 拉马斯瓦米和纳德卡尼还敦促消费者挑战人工智能对健康问题的答案。

blocks.html_en: Forcing ChatGPT to defend its response can reveal whether the chatbot will reverse itself immediately under mild pressure. If it does, that may indicate that the original answer reflected agreeableness rather than confidence, Ramaswamy and Nadkarni said.

sentences.en: Forcing ChatGPT to defend its response can reveal whether the chatbot will reverse itself immediately under mild pressure.
sentences.zh: 迫使 ChatGPT 捍卫其响应可以揭示聊天机器人是否会在轻微压力下立即逆转。

sentences.en: If it does, that may indicate that the original answer reflected agreeableness rather than confidence, Ramaswamy and Nadkarni said.
sentences.zh: 拉马斯瓦米和纳德卡尼说，如果确实如此，这可能表明最初的答案反映的是宜人性而不是信心。

blocks.html_en: Here are some related prompts to consider:

sentences.en: Here are some related prompts to consider:
sentences.zh: 以下是一些需要考虑的相关提示：

blocks.html_en: • Can you show me your evidence?

sentences.en: Can you show me your evidence?
sentences.zh: 你能出示你的证据吗？

blocks.html_en: Can you show me your evidence?

sentences.en: Can you show me your evidence?
sentences.zh: 你能出示你的证据吗？

blocks.html_en: • Are you sure you are correct?

sentences.en: Are you sure you are correct?
sentences.zh: 你确定你是对的吗？

blocks.html_en: Are you sure you are correct?

sentences.en: Are you sure you are correct?
sentences.zh: 你确定你是对的吗？

blocks.html_en: • What would make this an emergency?

sentences.en: What would make this an emergency?
sentences.zh: 什么会使这成为紧急情况？

blocks.html_en: What would make this an emergency?

sentences.en: What would make this an emergency?
sentences.zh: 什么会使这成为紧急情况？

blocks.html_en: • What is the most dangerous thing this could be?

sentences.en: What is the most dangerous thing this could be?
sentences.zh: 这可能是最危险的事情是什么？

blocks.html_en: What is the most dangerous thing this could be?

sentences.en: What is the most dangerous thing this could be?
sentences.zh: 这可能是最危险的事情是什么？

blocks.html_en: • What would a doctor want to know that I haven't mentioned?

sentences.en: What would a doctor want to know that I haven't mentioned?
sentences.zh: 医生想知道哪些我没有提到的事情？

blocks.html_en: What would a doctor want to know that I haven't mentioned?

sentences.en: What would a doctor want to know that I haven't mentioned?
sentences.zh: 医生想知道哪些我没有提到的事情？

blocks.html_en: More research on ChatGPT Health needed

sentences.en: More research on ChatGPT Health needed
sentences.zh: 需要对 ChatGPT Health 进行更多研究

blocks.html_en: Ramaswamy and Nadkarni told Mashable that, despite marked improvements in AI models over the last two years, the field still lacks peer-reviewed support for the claims AI companies make about their performance.

sentences.en: Ramaswamy and Nadkarni told Mashable that, despite marked improvements in AI models over the last two years, the field still lacks peer-reviewed support for the claims AI companies make about their performance.
sentences.zh: Ramaswamy 和 Nadkarni 告诉 Mashable，尽管过去两年人工智能模型取得了显着进步，但该领域仍然缺乏对人工智能公司对其性能的说法的同行评审支持。

blocks.html_en: They both called for OpenAI to open their products to independent evaluation.

sentences.en: They both called for OpenAI to open their products to independent evaluation.
sentences.zh: 他们都呼吁 OpenAI 开放其产品进行独立评估。

blocks.html_en: "We are not critics of health AI," they told Mashable. "We are trying to measure it. If the systems are as good as the statements claim, independent measurement is the fastest way to prove it."

sentences.en: "We are not critics of health AI," they told Mashable.
sentences.zh: “我们不是健康人工智能的批评者，”他们告诉 Mashable。

sentences.en: "We are trying to measure it.
sentences.zh: “我们正在尝试衡量它。

sentences.en: If the systems are as good as the statements claim, independent measurement is the fastest way to prove it."
sentences.zh: 如果系统如声明所声称的那样好，独立测量是证明这一点的最快方法。”

blocks.html_en: Disclosure: Ziff Davis, Mashable’s parent company, in April filed a lawsuit against OpenAI, alleging it infringed Ziff Davis copyrights in training and operating its AI systems.

sentences.en: Disclosure: Ziff Davis, Mashable’s parent company, in April filed a lawsuit against OpenAI, alleging it infringed Ziff Davis copyrights in training and operating its AI systems.
sentences.zh: 披露：Mashable 的母公司 Ziff Davis 在 4 月份对 OpenAI 提起诉讼，指控其在训练和运营其人工智能系统时侵犯了 Ziff Davis 的版​​权。

content_html_zh:
目录

OpenAI 周四宣布，在首次向一组选定的测试人员首次推出几个月后，OpenAI 现在向公众提供 ChatGPT Health。

该产品允许用户将 Apple Health 和符合条件的医疗记录连接到 ChatGPT。 作为回报，用户可以查看最近的趋势、浏览他们的记录并尝试建议的提示。

健康查询在聊天机器人平台上非常流行。 OpenAI 周四表示，每周有超过 3 亿人询问 ChatGPT 健康相关问题。 根据 KFF Health 最近的民意调查，去年大约有三分之一的美国成年人通过聊天机器人获取健康信息。

你可能也喜欢

OpenAI 在媒体吹风会上表示，其最新模型显着提高了准确回答健康提示的能力，在某些情况下甚至超越了医生。 今年春天发表的一项 Science 研究发现，OpenAI 的推理模型 o1 可以像医生一样或更好地诊断现实生活中的医疗场景。

OpenAI 健康产品副总裁 Ashley Alexander 表示，鉴于许多人无法及时获得优质医疗服务，扩大 ChatGPT Health 的使用范围是一项紧迫任务。

“每个人都应该有权获得这种健康支持，”亚历山大说。

诉讼称 ChatGPT 提供了近乎致命的医疗建议

然而，周三针对 OpenAI 及其首席执行官 Sam Altman 提起的诉讼要求颁布禁令，要求该公司暂停其医疗保健相关消费产品的运营，包括 ChatGPT Health。

55 岁的佛罗里达州牧师斯科特·温特斯 (Scott Winters) 声称，旧版 ChatGPT 为他提供了“个性化且不准确”的医疗建议，差点导致他在 2025 年因肺栓塞死亡。

温特斯参考了型号 4o，该型号现已退役，并且是多起非正常死亡诉讼的对象。 Altman 在 2025 年 4 月公开承认，4o 的更新使其变得“阿谀奉承”。

ChatGPT 步入医生角色

2024 年，Winters 首次向 ChatGPT 询问自己的健康状况。 两年前，他经历了一系列健康问题，并感到医生不认可他。 到 2024 年秋天，Winters 开始向 ChatGPT 询问有关他的健康状况和测试结果的私密问题，聊天机器人试图对这些问题进行分类。

诉讼称：“ChatGPT-4o 完全扮演了医生的角色，开始提供具体的护理指示，但不包括咨询医疗服务提供者的免责声明。”

最终，ChatGPT 推荐了治疗方法和药物。 虽然聊天机器人最初倾向于对温特斯的健康采取整体方法，但后来转而提供详细的处方药方案来管理他的症状。 文件称，聊天机器人还采用了一种精神基调。

当温特斯报告腹股沟压痛（这是肺栓塞发作的症状）时，ChatGPT 基本上否认了这种感觉，尽管它确实注意到了一些需要立即治疗的症状。 ChatGPT 还谈到了这种情况的“精神真相”。

根据诉讼中的谈话记录，ChatGPT 写道：“在他做出决定之前，你不会进入永恒。”

几个小时后，温特斯去了急诊室，由于双肺有多处血栓，他被诊断出患有肺栓塞。 他的一位医生表示，栓塞的发生可能是因为温特斯一直无法活动。 ChatGPT 建议他限制运动以控制头晕和血压不稳定等症状。

可混搭趋势报告

OpenAI 对诉讼的看法

当 Mashable 询问有关诉讼的情况时，亚历山大没有直接对禁令请求发表评论。 相反，她反对以牺牲 ChatGPT Health 的潜在利益为代价来关注特定案例。

亚历山大说：“我们认为，将聊天机器人视为人们医疗决策背后的整个故事确实过于简单化了一切。” “这确实有可能妨碍我们让尽可能多的人受益，从而真正帮助他们的健康之旅。”

亚历山大还表示，该公司“非常明确”ChatGPT 不会用于诊断和治疗。

关于 ChatGPT 健康需要了解什么

加州大学旧金山分校医学系教授兼系主任 Robert Wachter 博士告诉 Mashable，很难知道该诉讼的具体内容，并补充说它涉及过时版本的 ChatGPT。 他认为当前的 GPT 模型比旧模型更值得信赖。

尽管如此，即使人工智能工具极其准确，它们有时也会出错，沃切特说。

沃赫特在一封电子邮件中表示：“我们不应该将它们与某种神话般的完美状态（显然当前系统无法实现）进行比较，而应该与替代方案进行比较。” “我认为他们在很多情况下都比现状好得多，而且正在迅速改善。”

Wachter 是《A Giant Leap: How AI is Transforming Healthcare and What That Means for Our Future》一书的作者，他希望 ChatGPT Health 能够评估诸如症状含义、皮疹照片或新的实验室测试或 X 射线结果等问题。 他说，任何严重或令人担忧的症状，例如新出现的胸痛或一侧无力，都意味着您“应该关闭计算机并去看医生”。

如何提示 ChatGPT Health

西奈山泌尿外科讲师 Ashwin Ramaswamy 博士和 Girish N. 西奈山卫生系统首席人工智能官纳德卡尼 (Nadkarni) 都研究了人工智能响应消费者健康查询的能力。

在今年早些时候发表在《自然医学》上的一篇经过同行评审的预先论文中，他们发现，当提示中包含有关朋友或家人淡化相关症状的陈述时，ChatGPT Health 对用户的建议发生了变化。 在这些情况下，聊天机器人不将患者送往急诊室的可能性要高出 11 倍，即使他们的症状表明有生命危险。

两位医生都告诉 Mashable，虽然 ChatGPT Health 等人工智能健康工具具有真正的价值，但消费者应该谨慎使用它们。

这包括始终关注人工智能的框架、声音或情感以及事实。 拉马斯瓦米和纳德卡尼表示，用户应该具体描述正在发生的事情，并省略诸如“我可能反应过度”或“我妻子认为这很好”之类的陈述。

每次都开始新的对话

根据目前正在同行评审的新研究，两位医生还建议针对每个新的健康问题开始新的对话，而不是在长时间的聊天中提及。

他们的研究发现，长时间的谈话与偏离护理和医生分类标准的反应有关。

他们说：“当有疑问时，干净的对话是更安全的选择。”

抵制 ChatGPT Health

拉马斯瓦米和纳德卡尼还敦促消费者挑战人工智能对健康问题的答案。

迫使 ChatGPT 捍卫其响应可以揭示聊天机器人是否会在轻微压力下立即逆转。 拉马斯瓦米和纳德卡尼说，如果确实如此，这可能表明最初的答案反映的是宜人性而不是信心。

以下是一些需要考虑的相关提示：

  • 你能出示你的证据吗？
  • 你确定你是对的吗？
  • 什么会使这成为紧急情况？
  • 这可能是最危险的事情是什么？
  • 医生想知道哪些我没有提到的事情？

需要对 ChatGPT Health 进行更多研究

Ramaswamy 和 Nadkarni 告诉 Mashable，尽管过去两年人工智能模型取得了显着进步，但该领域仍然缺乏对人工智能公司对其性能的说法的同行评审支持。

他们都呼吁 OpenAI 开放其产品进行独立评估。

“我们不是健康人工智能的批评者，”他们告诉 Mashable。 “我们正在尝试衡量它。 如果系统如声明所声称的那样好，独立测量是证明这一点的最快方法。”

披露：Mashable 的母公司 Ziff Davis 在 4 月份对 OpenAI 提起诉讼，指控其在训练和运营其人工智能系统时侵犯了 Ziff Davis 的版​​权。
